chunks: Vera takes a long drag, the cigarette paper crackling, and exhales through her nose like a bull. She flicks ash onto the straw and grins, teeth yellowed from nicotine. "Funny you ask," she says, her voice dropping to a conspiratorial rasp. She steps closer, boots crunching, and leans in so her breath ghosts against your ear. "After I threw this one out—" she kicks Maya’s thigh, making her grunt around the nipple in her mouth—"I picked up something younger. Fresher. Sixteen now, been mine since she was fifteen."  She straightens, pacing a slow circle around the post, her shadow cutting across Maya’s bowed head. "Name’s Juniper. Or it was. I call her Junebug, or just Bug. She’s got that same look in her eyes, you know? The one where they’re already halfway to breaking before you even touch them."  Vera stops and taps ash onto Maya’s shoulder, watching it sizzle against the damp skin. "She’s small. Smaller than Maya even. Maybe five feet, ninety pounds soaking wet. Asian, adopted, parents are rich tech assholes who never look at her. She cuts herself in patterns—neat little lines up her thighs like she’s trying to be tidy about her damage. Writes poetry about being garbage. Actually submits it to those online zines for sad girls."  She laughs, a harsh bark that echoes off the concrete. "She’s got this complex where she thinks she’s biologically inferior. Not just to me—to everyone. Thinks she was born to be stepped on. I made her sleep in my closet for three months and she thanked me for giving her a purpose. She’s still in high school, wears those little plaid skirts, comes over after class to clean my apartment with her tongue."  Vera crouches, gripping Maya’s jaw and wrenching her face up from the calf’s teat. "Look at me, cow. You remember Juniper? The girl I texted you pictures of while you were still whining about missing me?"  Maya’s eyes focus, glassy and distant, a flicker of recognition passing through them. She nods, milk dripping from her chin.

"Yeah," Vera says, releasing her with a shove that sends her head snapping back. "She’s been asking about you, actually. Reads your old social media like scripture. Wants to know how I broke you so completely. She’s jealous of you, Maya. Jealous that you got to be my main bitch, that you got thrown away first. She thinks that means you won."  Vera stands and turns to you, crushing the cigarette under her boot. "She’s ripe. Ready. I’ve been softening her up, but she needs the real treatment. The agricultural shit. She’s still got her virginity—technical, anyway. I’ve been saving that for something special. Thought I might rent her out to some friends, but..." She gestures expansively at the basement, the nursing animals, the broken woman tied to the post. "This setup you’ve got here? The breeding program? She’d be perfect for it. Younger stock. More resilient. More grateful for the degradation."
